# testdata — Certificate fixtures

All files in this directory are generated by `generate.sh`.
Re-generate at any time from the repository root:

```bash
bash testdata/generate.sh
```

Requires: OpenSSL ≥ 3.0.

---

## File index

| File | Type | Key details | Password | What to verify |
|------|------|-------------|----------|----------------|
| `rsa.key.pem` | RSA private key (PKCS#8, unencrypted) | 2048-bit | — | `ParsePrivateKey` → algorithm `RSA` |
| `ec.key.pem` | EC private key (PKCS#8, unencrypted) | P-256 | — | `ParsePrivateKey` → algorithm `EC` |
| `rsa.key.enc.pem` | RSA private key (PKCS#8, encrypted) | 2048-bit, AES-256 | `keypassword` | `ParsePrivateKey` → algorithm `Encrypted` |
| `rsa.crt.pem` | X.509 PEM, self-signed | RSA 2048, CN=test-rsa | — | CN, self-signed flag, SHA-256 fingerprint |
| `ec.crt.pem` | X.509 PEM, self-signed | EC P-256, CN=test-ec | — | algorithm=EC |
| `rsa.crt.der` | X.509 DER, self-signed | RSA 2048, CN=test-rsa | — | `ParseX509DER` → same CN as `rsa.crt.pem` |
| `server.crt.pem` | X.509 PEM, self-signed, TLS server | RSA 2048, CN=example.com | — | SAN DNS/IP present, EKU=ServerAuth |
| `client.crt.pem` | X.509 PEM, self-signed, TLS client | RSA 2048, CN=client | — | SAN email present, EKU=ClientAuth |
| `expired.crt.pem` | X.509 PEM, self-signed, expired | RSA 2048, validity 2020 | — | `CERT_EXPIRED` issue raised |
| `ca.crt.pem` | X.509 PEM, root CA | RSA 2048, CN=Test Root CA, CA:TRUE | — | `IsSelfSigned`, CA flag |
| `leaf.crt.pem` | X.509 PEM, leaf signed by `ca.crt.pem` | RSA 2048, CN=leaf.example.com | — | Issuer matches `ca.crt.pem` subject |
| `intermediate.crt.pem` | X.509 PEM, intermediate CA | RSA 2048, signed by root CA, CA:TRUE | — | `IsCA` flag, issuer=root CA |
| `leaf-int.crt.pem` | X.509 PEM, leaf signed by intermediate | RSA 2048, SAN DNS, EKU=ServerAuth | — | 3-level chain |
| `bundle.pem` | PEM bundle — leaf + root CA | concatenated PEM blocks | — | `Detect` → `TypeBundle`, 2 certs |
| `fullchain.pem` | PEM bundle — leaf-int + intermediate + root | 3 concatenated certs | — | `Detect` → `TypeBundle`, 3 certs |
| `rsa.csr.pem` | PKCS#10 CSR | RSA 2048, CN=test-csr | — | `ParseCSR` → CN, no signature error |
| `ec.csr.pem` | PKCS#10 CSR, ECDSA | EC P-256, CN=ec-csr.example.com | — | `ParseCSR` → algorithm EC |
| `test.p12` | PKCS#12 — RSA cert + key | RSA 2048 | `testpassword` | `IsBinaryPKCS12`=true; wrong password → error |
| `test-nopass.p12` | PKCS#12 — RSA cert + key, no password | RSA 2048 | *(empty)* | `ParsePKCS12("", ...)` → success |
| `test.crl.pem` | CRL signed by `ca.crt.pem` | — | — | `ParseCRL` → issuer matches CA |

---

## Key / cert relationships

```
ca.crt.pem  (root CA, self-signed)
├── leaf.crt.pem        (signed by root CA)
└── intermediate.crt.pem (intermediate CA, signed by root CA)
    └── leaf-int.crt.pem (signed by intermediate CA)
```

`bundle.pem`    = `leaf.crt.pem` + `ca.crt.pem`
`fullchain.pem` = `leaf-int.crt.pem` + `intermediate.crt.pem` + `ca.crt.pem`

---

## Notes

- Private key files (`*.key.pem`, `*.key.enc.pem`) are **not** committed to version
  control in production usage; they are present here for testing purposes only.
- The `expired.crt.pem` certificate has a validity window of 2020-01-01 to
  2020-12-31 and is deliberately expired.
- `test.crl.pem` may be absent if the local `openssl ca` configuration is
  incomplete; tests that require it are guarded with `t.Skip`.
